DISCOVERY, DEPT. 525, AS TO THE DEC-05-2024 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF CHETAN PATIL, AND BLUE VALLEY VENTURES LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTION IS MOOT BECAUSE DEFENDANT HAS SERVED CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO THE DISCOVERY AT ISSUE. NO SANCTIONS. THE AMOUNT OF THE SANCTIONS MUST BE GROUNDED IN FACTS: THE HOURS SPENT, THE RATE OF ATTORNEYS' FEES, THE REASONABLENESS OF THE FEES, AND THE COSTS EXPENDED. COUNSEL MICHAEL WALKER IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, AND EMAIL IT TO JUDGE PRO TEM TOM COHEN AT TOM@THOMASCOHEN.COM .JUDGE PRO TEM: TOM COHEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. =(525/JPT)

MOTION TO BIFURCATE/SEVER / Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate Plaintiffs' Claims Into Separate Trials
Off Calendar Aug-27-2025 Continued To Apr-13-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Discovery
Off Calendar Sep-19-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (by Zoom):
Mike Chappars, Esq. 408-508-447 for Plaintiff.
Brandon Ramcharan, Esq. 415-262-8584, Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard and Smith for Defendant.

ARGUED. The Court announces the tentative ruling and states the request to take the matter off calendar yesterday was denied. The Court shares its views. Mr. Ramcharan states counsel has substantially complied and did not ask for sanctions. Mr. Chappars informs the court responses were provided the day after the motion was filed, agreed to continue the trial and Defense counsel stated they were withdrawing the motion. After full argument, the Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT GENIUS CONSTRUCTION, LLC.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Discovery.

Defendant Genius Construction, LLC's Motion To Compel Plaintiff's [sic] Responses To Discovery is Taken Under Submission.***

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After the hearing an order re Defendant's motion to compel plaintiffs' responses to discovery is filed. (see scanned order for details)***

MOTION TO BIFURCATE/SEVER / Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate Plaintiffs' Claims Into Separate Trials
Off Calendar Jul-28-2025 Continued To Oct-06-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs GENIUS CONSTRUCTION, LLC, MOHAMMED ELEMAIN, NASSIF AYYAD, SAGA ELEMAIN's Motion To Bifurcate Plaintiffs' Claims Into Separate Trials.

The motion to bifurcate is TRANSFERRED to Department 206 on the day of trial, October 6, 2025, to be heard by the assigned trial judge.

Judge: Garrett L. Wong; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/GLW)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-15-2024 Set For Trial On Oct-06-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion To Compel Further Responses
Off Calendar Dec-05-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. MIKE CHAPPARS, Esq. of Law Office of Mike Chappars (tel: 408-508-3447) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. MICHAEL WALKER, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (tel: 415-362-2580) for Defendants.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF CHETAN PATIL, AND BLUE VALLEY VENTURES LLC's Motion To Compel Further Responses.

Plaintiff contests the tentative ruling. Both parties stipulate to Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen, and authorize the clerk to sign the stipulation on their behalf. Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The Motion is moot because Defendant has served code compliant responses to the discovery at issue.
No sanctions. The amount of the sanctions must be grounded in facts: the hours spent, the rate of attorneys' fees, the reasonableness of the fees, and the costs expended.

Counsel Michael Walker is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, and email it to Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen at tom@thomascohen.com .

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. =(525/JPT)
